lessons.title: 1.3 MORALITY AND VALUE
topics: Morals are societal norms that dictate what is considered right and wrong within a community.
Values, on the other hand, are guiding principles, standards or behaviour that are regarded as
desirable or held in high esteem in society. Morality is a set of rules and beliefs that distinguish
good behaviour from bad behaviour. Morality refers to the principles and values that guide
activities: Cluster the learners in small groups to discuss the concept of morality
Each group to report key ideas from the discussion to the class
Key ideas to be grouped and written down on the board
Learners to connect the ideas and come up with the definition of morality
Teacher to consolidate the meaning of morality on the board
resources: Learners to present their findings in class
Teacher to consolidate and summarise the findings of the research on the board
Class discusses solutions to different moral dilemmas
 Teachers to make a summary of meaningful points
assessment: Differentiate the following terms: moral, amoral and immoral.

lessons.title: 1.4 CHOOSING AND TALENTS
topics: Choosing and talents are like the two sides of a coin as they cannot be separated. They both
play important roles in people’s lives. Life is full of choices, as a result people make choices
throughout their life time. Some of the choices people make are good and others are bad, hence
we have to follow all the necessary steps in order to make good choices. Choices can be made
activities: have been designed to promote practical demonstrations with a view that learners will develop
competences that will help them in life.
In this topic, there are three sub-topics. These are Choices, Choices in the four religions in
Zambia and Talents. From the presentations, teachers are expected to gain understanding and
skills in order to present the topic effectively and confidently.
